description_long: This is a vintage, industrial-style metal locker cabinet, painted in a faded turquoise-green color with visible patches of rust and wear, suggesting long-term use in an urban or industrial environment. The front features two tall doors, each with two rectangular ventilation grilles and a small, simple handle on the left door. The cabinet stands on four sturdy, straight legs, elevating it slightly off the ground. The sides and back are plain, with the same worn paint and some rust along the edges and corners. The overall construction appears robust, likely made from sheet metal, and the doors are likely hinged for access to the interior storage space. This type of cabinet is commonly found in garages, workshops, schools, or public facilities for secure storage of personal items or tools.
description: A weathered, turquoise-green metal locker cabinet with two vented doors and visible rust, standing on four legs. Its industrial look suggests use in urban or workshop settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing two vented doors with handles and visible rust patches.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain, flat surface with worn paint and no visible features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a seamless, flat back panel with rust along the edges.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to the other side, with plain surface and minor rust.
